text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Male
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
REFERRING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED]
SERVICE: Urology / Surgical Oncology

PROCEDURE: Open right radical nephrectomy with en bloc excision of Gerota fascia, right adrenalectomy, and template retroperitoneal lymph node dissection
INDICATION: Metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (Stage IV; T3bN1M1), cytoreductive intent

------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) RECEIVED
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen A: Right radical nephrectomy with perinephric fat and Gerota fascia (en bloc)
Specimen B: Right adrenal gland
Specimen C: Retroperitoneal lymph node dissection (labeled "right para-aortic, aortocaval, and paracaval lymph nodes")

All specimens received fresh, then placed in 10% neutral buffered formalin. Specimen A received with a surgical suture marking the superior pole. Chain of custody intact.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY
------------------------------------------------------------------------
The patient is a 63-year-old male with biopsy-proven clear cell renal cell carcinoma (WHO/ISUP grade 4, sarcomatoid differentiation approximately 20% by prior core needle biopsy) of the right kidney. Pre-operative imaging demonstrated an 11.4 cm heterogeneous right renal mass with extension into the right renal vein (without IVC involvement), perinephric fat involvement by imaging, and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. Bilateral pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease are present. Pre-operative biopsy (SP-XXXX-XXXX) showed PAX8+, CA-IX diffusely+, CD10+, vimentin+, CK7 focally+, TFE3-. This specimen is submitted following multidisciplinary tumor board recommendation for cytoreductive nephrectomy. Comprehensive genomic profiling (Foundation One CDx) from prior biopsy demonstrated VHL p.L89fs*4, PBRM1 p.Q357*, BAP1 p.R227*, SETD2 p.R1625*, CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion, and MET amplification, consistent with aggressive clear cell RCC biology.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A -- RIGHT RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY WITH GEROTA FASCIA:
Received fresh then fixed: The specimen consists of a right kidney with attached perinephric adipose tissue within Gerota fascia, and a segment of right renal vein with associated soft tissue. The kidney with surrounding perinephric fat and Gerota fascia together measure 18.5 x 14.2 x 12.0 cm and weigh 1,284 grams (fresh weight). The Gerota fascia is intact over the majority of the specimen surface. A surgical suture designates the superior pole.

The renal vein is present, measures 3.8 cm in length x 1.2 cm in diameter. Upon opening the renal vein longitudinally, a firm, tan-gray intraluminal thrombus is identified extending from the hilum to the surgical resection margin of the vein. The thrombus measures 3.1 cm in length. The vascular resection margin is designated and taken en face. The ureteral margin is identified, measures 0.4 cm in diameter; grossly free of tumor.

Serial bread-loafing of the kidney in the coronal plane reveals a large, poorly circumscribed, heterogeneous mass occupying the upper and mid poles of the kidney, measuring 11.6 x 10.2 x 9.7 cm. The cut surface of the mass is variegated: areas of tan-yellow to golden-yellow fleshy tissue alternating with extensive zones of geographic necrosis (grayish-white, friable material) comprising more than half of the mass cross-sectional area. Focal areas of hemorrhage are also noted (dark red). A firm, gray-white area in the upper pole region, measuring approximately 2.6 x 1.8 cm, has a distinctly different, dense, whorled texture compared to the remainder of the tumor; this area is designated the "sarcomatoid focus" and is differentially sampled. The mass appears to involve and efface the renal sinus fat at the hilum. The mass does not grossly appear to penetrate through Gerota fascia; Gerota fascia inked and submitted. The remaining uninvolved renal parenchyma is tan-brown, smooth, with a normal corticomedullary ratio; no additional cortical nodules or cysts identified. The kidney capsule is largely intact.

Inking: Superior (green), inferior (blue), anterior (yellow), posterior (black).

Sections submitted:
A1: Renal vein vascular resection margin, en face
A2: Renal vein thrombus, proximal segment
A3: Renal vein thrombus, mid segment
A4: Junction of tumor and renal vein / renal sinus
A5-A6: Sarcomatoid focus (gray-white, firm area), entirely submitted (2 blocks)
A7-A14: Representative sections of viable tumor and necrosis interface, various quadrants (8 blocks)
A15: Tumor interface with renal sinus fat (hilar)
A16: Tumor with perinephric fat (anterior)
A17: Tumor with perinephric fat (posterior)
A18: Tumor with Gerota fascia (superior)
A19: Gerota fascia, uninvolved area (to evaluate for microscopic invasion)
A20: Ureteral margin and proximal ureter
A21: Uninvolved renal parenchyma (lower pole)
A22-A23: Additional viable tumor for potential IHC / biomarker studies

SPECIMEN B -- RIGHT ADRENAL GLAND:
The specimen is a right adrenal gland with periglandular adipose tissue, measuring 5.2 x 3.1 x 1.8 cm and weighing 8.6 grams. The external surface is smooth and tan-yellow with a small amount of adherent adipose tissue. Serial sectioning reveals the normal golden-yellow cortex and gray-white medulla. No discrete nodules, masses, or areas of hemorrhage/necrosis are identified. No gross tumor involvement.

Sections submitted:
B1-B2: Adrenal gland, representative sections including cortex and medulla

SPECIMEN C -- RETROPERITONEAL LYMPH NODE DISSECTION:
Multiple fragments of fibrofatty tissue submitted en bloc, measuring in aggregate 12.4 x 8.3 x 2.4 cm. Upon careful dissection, 8 lymph nodes are identified. Lymph nodes range from 0.5 cm to 2.9 cm in greatest dimension. Three lymph nodes (two of the larger nodes, 2.9 cm and 2.1 cm) appear grossly replaced by firm, tan-white to grayish nodular tissue with areas of central necrosis, consistent with metastatic involvement. The remaining five lymph nodes appear grossly reactive (0.5 cm to 1.2 cm; soft, homogeneous, tan).

Sections submitted:
C1-C3: Enlarged lymph nodes with suspected metastatic involvement (one each per block)
C4-C8: Remaining five lymph nodes, one per cassette
C9: Additional perinodal fibrofatty soft tissue (for completeness)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A -- RIGHT RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY:

PRIMARY TUMOR: Sections confirm a high-grade clear cell renal cell carcinoma with a predominantly alveolar/nested architecture and areas of solid sheet-like growth, punctuated by extensive geographic necrosis comprising greater than 50% of the total tumor cross-sectional area. The clear cell component demonstrates cells with abundant pale to vacuolated cytoplasm and eccentrically placed nuclei; the highest-grade conventional clear cell areas show prominent, irregular, cherry-red macronucleoli readily visible at 10x objective, consistent with WHO/ISUP grade 4. Nuclear pleomorphism is marked, with multinucleated and bizarre giant tumor cells scattered throughout.

SARCOMATOID DIFFERENTIATION: The densely cellular, spindle-cell component identified grossly is confirmed on microscopic examination. This sarcomatoid component consists of elongated to ovoid cells with markedly pleomorphic hyperchromatic nuclei, conspicuous nucleoli, scant-to-moderate eosinophilic cytoplasm, and a high mitotic rate (up to 11 mitoses per 10 high-power fields, including atypical forms). The sarcomatoid foci are arranged in fascicles and storiform patterns without convincing differentiation toward a specific heterologous element. The sarcomatoid component is estimated to comprise 22% of the viable tumor volume on systematic sampling. Transition zones between the conventional clear cell component and the sarcomatoid component are identified. Rhabdoid differentiation is not identified. No heterologous differentiation (e.g., osteosarcomatous, chondrosarcomatous, or rhabdomyosarcomatous elements) is present.

TUMOR NECROSIS: Extensive geographic coagulative necrosis is present, comprising greater than 50% of the total sampled tumor area. Necrotic ghost outlines of tumor cells are present.

LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: Present. Numerous lymphovascular spaces within and at the periphery of the tumor are involved by tumor emboli.

RENAL VEIN THROMBUS: Confirmed. The renal vein contains a non-occlusive to occlusive intraluminal tumor thrombus that extends from the renal sinus to the vascular resection margin. See margin status below.

RENAL SINUS FAT INVASION: Present. Tumor is identified infiltrating the renal sinus adipose tissue at the hilum.

PERINEPHRIC FAT INVASION: Present. Tumor infiltrates the perinephric adipose tissue (pT3a criterion confirmed in addition to renal vein involvement).

GEROTA FASCIA: Not involved. Gerota fascia sections show fibroadipose tissue without tumor involvement; the deep surface of Gerota fascia does not show tumor penetration.

PERINEURAL INVASION: Absent.

SURGICAL MARGINS:
- Vascular (renal vein) resection margin: POSITIVE. Tumor thrombus extends to the inked vascular margin (margin 0 mm).
- Ureteral margin: Negative for carcinoma.
- Perinephric soft tissue margins (Gerota fascia): Negative; all inked Gerota fascia surfaces are free of tumor.

UNINVOLVED KIDNEY: The uninvolved renal parenchyma (lower pole, Block A21) shows changes consistent with diabetic nephropathy: mild to moderate mesangial expansion and diffuse glomerular basement membrane thickening. Mild arteriolar hyalinosis is present, consistent with hypertensive nephrosclerosis. No additional foci of carcinoma or papillary adenomas identified. No nephrolithiasis.

SPECIMEN B -- RIGHT ADRENAL GLAND:
The adrenal gland shows normal cortex with three distinct zones (glomerulosa, fasciculata, reticularis) and normal medulla. No involvement by metastatic renal cell carcinoma. Cortical nodule (micronodule, 0.2 cm) of adrenocortical cells is present, consistent with a benign incidental cortical adenoma. No adrenal cortical hyperplasia.

SPECIMEN C -- RETROPERITONEAL LYMPH NODES:
Of 8 lymph nodes identified, 3 are involved by metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid features (3/8 positive). The three positive nodes (Blocks C1, C2, C3; 2.9 cm, 2.1 cm, and 1.7 cm) show partial to complete effacement of nodal architecture by sheets of neoplastic cells displaying both conventional clear cell and sarcomatoid morphology. Areas of necrosis are present within the nodal metastases. Extranodal extension is present in the largest positive node (Block C1; tumor extends beyond the nodal capsule into perinodal adipose tissue). The remaining five lymph nodes are negative for malignancy (reactive follicular hyperplasia).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Performed on representative sections of Specimen A (primary tumor -- sarcomatoid component, Blocks A5-A6; conventional clear cell component, Block A7):

Panel on sarcomatoid component (Block A5):
- Vimentin: Diffusely positive (3+)
- Desmin: Focally positive (1+, scattered cells)
- CK AE1/AE3 (pan-cytokeratin): Focally positive (1+, rare cells)
- PAX8: Focally positive (1-2+, subset of cells at sarcomatoid-clear cell interface)
- CA-IX: Negative in sarcomatoid component; diffusely positive (3+) in adjacent conventional clear cell component
- SMA (smooth muscle actin): Focally positive (1+, focal)
- S100: Negative
- SOX10: Negative
- Myogenin: Negative
- CD34: Positive in background stroma/vessels; negative in tumor cells

Panel on clear cell component (Block A7):
- PAX8: Diffusely positive (3+)
- CA-IX: Diffusely positive (3+, membranous pattern)
- CD10: Diffusely positive (3+)
- Vimentin: Positive (2+)
- CK7: Focally positive (1+)
- TFE3: Negative (no nuclear staining; excludes MiT family translocation RCC)
- TFE3 FISH: [PENDING -- results to be addended]

PD-L1 (22C3 antibody, Dako PharmDx platform):
- Conventional clear cell component: Combined Positive Score (CPS): 14; Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 8%
- Sarcomatoid component (Block A5): CPS: 22; TPS: 18%
- Combined (overall): CPS approximately 18 (increased expression in sarcomatoid foci)

MMR (mismatch repair) proteins:
- MLH1: Retained nuclear expression
- MSH2: Retained nuclear expression
- MSH6: Retained nuclear expression
- PMS2: Retained nuclear expression
- Interpretation: No evidence of mismatch repair protein loss (consistent with MSS tumor on prior molecular testing)

Ki-67 (MIB-1) proliferation index:
- Conventional clear cell component: approximately 30-35%
- Sarcomatoid component: approximately 55-60% (markedly elevated)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
------------------------------------------------------------------------
TFE3 FISH (fluorescence in situ hybridization): PENDING -- to rule out TFE3 gene rearrangement in context of focal TFE3 IHC negativity (low pretest probability given CPS/IHC profile, but performed for completeness). Results will be addended to this report upon receipt.

Tumor bank/biorepository: Frozen tissue and FFPE tumor blocks (Blocks A5, A7, A22, A23) have been accessioned for potential future molecular testing at the discretion of the treating oncologist. Chain of custody maintained.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSES
------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. RIGHT RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY WITH GEROTA FASCIA:
   CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, WHO/ISUP NUCLEAR GRADE 4 (highest grade present)
   - Sarcomatoid differentiation: PRESENT, comprising approximately 22% of viable tumor volume
   - Rhabdoid differentiation: Absent
   - Tumor size: 11.6 x 10.2 x 9.7 cm
   - Tumor necrosis: EXTENSIVE (>50% of sampled tumor area)
   - Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT
   - Perineural invasion: Absent
   - Renal vein involvement: PRESENT (tumor thrombus)
   - Renal sinus fat invasion: PRESENT
   - Perinephric fat invasion: PRESENT
   - Gerota fascia involvement: ABSENT
   - Adrenal gland (Specimen B): Not involved (see Specimen B)
   - SURGICAL MARGINS:
       Vascular (renal vein) resection margin: POSITIVE (tumor at margin)
       Ureteral resection margin: Negative
       Perinephric soft tissue/Gerota fascia margins: Negative
   - pT stage: pT3b (tumor extends into renal vein)
   - Uninvolved kidney: Changes consistent with diabetic nephropathy and hypertensive nephrosclerosis

B. RIGHT ADRENAL GLAND:
   NO TUMOR INVOLVEMENT
   - Incidental benign adrenocortical micronodule (0.2 cm)

C. RETROPERITONEAL LYMPH NODE DISSECTION:
   METASTATIC CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA WITH SARCOMATOID FEATURES
   - 3 of 8 lymph nodes positive (3/8)
   - Extranodal extension: PRESENT (largest positive node, 2.9 cm)
   - pN stage: pN1

OVERALL PATHOLOGIC STAGE (AJCC 8th Edition, post-cytoreductive nephrectomy):
   ypT3b  pN1  M1
   (M1 stage retained per clinical staging; bilateral pulmonary metastases documented on pre-operative imaging)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
------------------------------------------------------------------------
This nephrectomy specimen confirms the pre-operative biopsy findings of high-grade clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation. The sarcomatoid component has increased very slightly from the biopsy estimate of 20% to 22% on whole-specimen systematic sampling, consistent with sampling variability. The extensive coagulative necrosis (>50%) and the elevated Ki-67 proliferation index within the sarcomatoid component are consistent with highly aggressive tumor biology. Co-expression of both PAX8 positivity (focal) in the sarcomatoid areas and diffuse CA-IX positivity in the conventional component supports the interpretation of sarcomatoid transformation of a clear cell renal cell carcinoma rather than a primary renal sarcoma. The detection of elevated PD-L1 expression (CPS 22) particularly in the sarcomatoid component is consistent with prior comprehensive genomic profiling (BAP1 loss, which has been associated with sarcomatoid differentiation and higher PD-L1 expression in ccRCC). The positive vascular (renal vein) resection margin reflects the extent of venous thrombus identified pre-operatively and intraoperatively; this finding has been communicated to the surgical team. Extranodal extension in the largest metastatic lymph node is noted. TFE3 FISH results will be addended upon receipt.

Case reviewed by attending surgical pathologist. Digital slides reviewed. Gross and microscopic findings correlate.

Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED], M.D.
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

Report status: FINAL (with PENDING addendum for TFE3 FISH)
